resiliencePolicy: If an owner is unavailable for more than one working block or blocks the critical path, backupOwner may take over after recording reassignment.
closurePolicy: No sprint may be closed until acceptance criteria, base tests, sprint-specific tests, docs/handoff, and Oreshnik zone checks pass. See docs/obsidian-vault/METODOLOGIA/ORQUESTACION_AGENTES_HEPTACORE.md.
currentExecutionOrder: Wave 1 parallel: Jean S-HC-PROD-02, Manuel S-HC-PROD-03 (LLM provider adapter + Turpial tenant QA)
Wave 2 parallel: Jean S-HC-PROD-04, Manuel S-HC-PROD-05 after S-HC-PROD-03
Wave 3 parallel: Jean S-HC-PROD-09/S-HC-PROD-11, Manuel S-HC-PROD-07/S-HC-PROD-08/S-HC-PROD-10 after core proof
Wave 4 sequential: S-HC-PROD-06 control bus/dashboard after PROD-02/03/04/05/07/08/09/10/11
Final: S-HC-RELEASE-01 end-to-end Turpial Sound production proof

tasks.title: Production DB/Auth/env and Turpial seed smoke
acceptance: Production env checklist completed without committing secrets
Prisma migrations validated/applied to hosted DB
Admin and Turpial seed verified
Login smoke passes against hosted DB
29 drafts and 46 assets verified for turpial-sound
tasks.handoff: Jean owns DB/Auth/env proof. Manuel does not touch packages/db or auth during this sprint unless double lock is requested.

history.reason: User-directed PROD closeout split: Jean owns production DB/Auth/env path.

tasks.title: LLM provider adapter plus Turpial tenant functional QA and UX polish
acceptance: LLM provider adapter interface exists with deterministic fallback for offline/dev
Initial provider is selected by environment variable without committing secrets
Tenant-specific strategy generation can consume Turpial intake/assets/context
Generated strategy output is structured and auditable before drafts are approved
Tenant console renders strategy, assets, queue, calendar, reports and readiness
Approve/reject flow works in UI
No overlap with Jean DB/Auth/env work
Manual user path documented for Turpial Sound
tasks.handoff: Manuel owns packages/agents and tenant UX/product QA. Jean should not edit packages/agents, dashboard-console or admin-console during this sprint.

tasks.title: Worker, Redis and persistent dry-run processing
acceptance: Redis/worker hosting plan and configuration documented
Worker validates with Turpial queue
Dry-run job path emits logs without real RRSS publish
No Vercel serverless long-running worker assumption remains
tasks.handoff: Jean owns worker/integrations. Manuel should not edit apps/worker or packages/integrations while this is active.

tasks.title: Publishing gate UI, AuditLog and rollback proof
acceptance: Dry-run gate is clear and cannot publish real RRSS
Approved draft can move to SCHEDULED through UI
AuditLog entry is visible in reports/admin activity
Rollback procedure is documented and tested
tasks.handoff: Manuel owns web publishing gate. Jean only consumes the endpoint/contract from worker side.

tasks.title: Oreshnik operator dashboard and canonical task board
acceptance: Task board reflects PROD reality
Operator dashboard/report shows current branch, mother, events and owners
No stale Jean/Manuel sprint ambiguity remains
Closeout process produces one canonical release handoff
tasks.handoff: Manuel owns Oreshnik/control bus. Jean should not edit Oreshnik scripts without explicit double lock.

tasks.title: Sales landing, client onboarding and login entry
acceptance: Public landing explains HeptaCore value, tenant operating model, approvals and paid-growth guardrails
Landing has clear CTA to login/onboarding without exposing tenant console publicly
Client onboarding path captures business, offer, audience, networks, assets and approval preferences
Login remains protected and usable after landing replaces root redirect
No marketing claim promises guaranteed sales or unsupervised automation
tasks.handoff: Manuel owns public web and onboarding UX. Jean should not edit landing/login while this sprint is active.

tasks.title: Draft editor and post modification workflow
acceptance: Operator can edit draft title, caption, CTA, hashtags, risk/review flag and scheduled date before approval
Edits are tenant-scoped and RBAC-protected
Edited draft returns to review state when content changes after approval
AuditLog records before/after metadata for post modification
UI makes edit, approve, reject and dry-run states distinct
tasks.handoff: Manuel owns draft editor in web UI/API. Jean consumes final draft state from worker, no simultaneous edits in drafts API.

tasks.title: Paid ads campaign engine with 35 percent overhead gate
acceptance: Campaign spend remains blocked unless explicitly approved
Campaign plan includes platform budget, 35 percent overhead, total client charge and approval state
No real campaign spend can execute from default env
Worker/integration contract supports dry-run campaign creation
Compliance notes explain platform spend, HeptaCore overhead and rollback/no-spend proof
tasks.handoff: Jean owns worker/integration campaign engine. Manuel will own web campaign UI separately.

tasks.title: Paid ads management UI and tenant billing surface
acceptance: Tenant can view campaign proposals, platform spend, 35 percent overhead and total charge
Operator can approve/reject paid campaign proposal without triggering real spend
Admin can see paid-growth status per tenant
AuditLog records paid campaign approval decisions
UI labels real spend as blocked until explicit production unlock
tasks.handoff: Manuel owns web paid ads UI after Jean defines worker/campaign contract.

tasks.title: Paid scraper compliance and controlled discovery adapter
acceptance: No real scraping is enabled by default
Discovery adapter supports mock/dry-run and documents paid provider requirements
Sensitive action gate covers paid scraping with explicit approval
Compliance matrix documents allowed sources, forbidden scraping and tenant consent
Output can feed strategy context without violating hard stops
tasks.handoff: Jean owns integrations/discovery adapter. Any web surface must wait for contract or be assigned to Manuel in a later sprint.

tasks.title: Canonical task board and derived docs alignment gate
acceptance: Preflight blocks when derived docs contradict var/oreshnik/task-board.json
Canonical derived docs can be regenerated from task-board
Reassign regenerates derived docs after owner changes
Close marks task-board item done and regenerates derived docs
Zone map covers var/oreshnik canonical state
tasks.handoff: Manuel owns Oreshnik alignment infrastructure. Jean consumes preflight output and must not proceed when canonical drift blocks.

history.description: alineacion definitiva task board docs derivados

tasks.title: Mandatory remote push on every Oreshnik close
acceptance: oreshnik:close always pushes the operator child branch
oreshnik:close always pushes the generated MADRE branch
Closure no longer depends on --push for remote visibility
Jean and Manuel can align from the latest remote MADRE after every sprint
tasks.handoff: After close, the remote child branch and remote MADRE branch are the synchronization source for both operators.

tasks.title: Mandatory remote fast-forward and divergence gate in preflight
acceptance: Preflight fetches origin and compares current branch with origin/current-branch
Preflight fast-forwards automatically when local branch is safely behind and working tree is clean
Preflight blocks when local branch is behind with local changes
Preflight blocks when local and remote branch diverged
Preflight blocks when a newer remote MADRE exists than the declared canonical mother
tasks.handoff: Both operators must start from preflight; remote branch and latest MADRE drift are detected before any implementation work.

tasks.title: End-to-end Turpial Sound production proof
acceptance: Login works
Sales landing and onboarding entry work
Turpial tenant loads strategy and assets
Draft can be edited before approval
Draft approval works
Dry-run publish proof works
Paid ads proposal shows 35 percent overhead without real spend
Paid scraper/discovery remains gated and documented
AuditLog/report proves the action
typecheck, build and worker:validate pass
Real RRSS publish remains blocked unless explicitly unlocked
tasks.handoff: Final release must be sequential or pair-reviewed; no parallel edits in release sprint.

User-directed reassignment: Manuel owns web publishing gate.
